script:
Hello, I am Josh Weinberg and this is The Portfolio Podcast. Wednesday, as of July 1 2026 at 5:02 AM ET — here is your QQQM recap. The portfolio closed at one hundred three thousand one hundred twenty-five dollars and forty-nine cents, up three thousand one hundred twenty-five dollars and forty-nine cents on the session. That is a gain of three point one three percent from the one hundred thousand dollar base.

Tesla. Alphabet. Two names that dominated Wednesday's session and reminded investors exactly why megacap technology remains the engine of this portfolio. Tesla, T-S-L-A, surged ten point seven seven percent in a single session. Alphabet, G-O-O-G-L, added five point nine two percent. Combined, those two moves alone accounted for the lion's share of Wednesday's portfolio advance.

Broad market conditions Wednesday were constructive across technology and communication services, with the Nasdaq Composite posting a session that reflected broad strength in megacap names. Artificial intelligence infrastructure spending continued to act as the primary tailwind, with a Reuters analysis from finance.yahoo.com noting that technology stocks may have more room to run than energy shares as AI spending remains elevated and oil prices retreat. On the macro side, Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh made headlines after removing the forward guidance framework that Wall Street has relied on since two thousand three, a development that rattled bond desks and introduced a new layer of uncertainty around rate expectations heading into the second half of twenty twenty-six.

The top story Wednesday belongs to Alphabet, G-O-O-G-L, and it is a two-sided coin. On the upside, the company closed at three hundred fifty-seven dollars and thirty-seven cents, up nineteen dollars and ninety-eight cents, a gain of five point nine two percent — making it the second-best performer in the portfolio by percentage move. The catalyst driving that rally appears tied to the broader artificial intelligence infrastructure build-out. Reporting from finance.yahoo.com cited data center construction across the Middle East growing at a compound annual growth rate of twenty-five point seven nine percent from twenty twenty-five to twenty thirty-one, with Google named as one of the major investment anchors in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Israel. Additionally, SpaceX, now listed on the Nasdaq under the ticker N-A-S-D-A-Q-G-S colon S-P-C-X, disclosed AI compute agreements valued at nearly twenty-eight billion dollars annually, with Google listed as one of its key AI infrastructure partners. That is a meaningful commercial validation of Alphabet's positioning in the neocloud ecosystem. The shadow on the session, however, was a report from finance.yahoo.com citing South Korea's antitrust regulator, the Korea Fair Trade Commission, alleging that Google abused its dominant position in the Android app marketplace between July twenty nineteen and March twenty twenty-six. The bureau found that Google's internal program, known as Project Hug, offered game developers financial incentives to favor Google's app store over competitors. The affected revenue figure cited in the examiner's report was fourteen point one six trillion South Korean won, equivalent to approximately nine point one billion US dollars. Corrective measures and a financial penalty are expected to follow. For G-O-O-G-L holders in this portfolio, at eight point zero one percent weight, the regulatory overhang is real — but Wednesday's market verdict was clear: the AI tailwind won the day.

Turning now to the full holdings rundown. Apple, A-A-P-L, closed at two hundred eighty-nine dollars and thirty-six cents, up five dollars and fifty-eight cents, a gain of one point nine seven percent on Wednesday's session. The headline out of Brussels was cautiously constructive. European Technology Commissioner Henna Virkkunen held a video call with Apple Chief Executive Tim Cook on Monday, and a Commission spokesperson described the conversation as constructive, noting that work continues on topics of common interest. The underlying friction remains unresolved — Apple has stated that its Siri artificial intelligence features will not launch initially in the EU on iPhones or iPads, with each side blaming the other for the impasse around interoperability standards. For the largest position in this portfolio at eight point nine seven percent, the EU regulatory situation is a slow-moving but material risk to Apple's addressable AI market in Europe. Separately, DoubleLine Deputy Chief Investment Officer Jeffrey Sherman, as cited by finance.yahoo.com, flagged what he called excesses in the market driven by heavy AI spending, and expressed a defensive posture in bond portfolios. That macro caution is worth noting as a potential headwind for rate-sensitive technology valuations.

Over at Microsoft, M-S-F-T, the session was essentially flat. The stock closed at three hundred seventy-three dollars and two cents, up five cents, a gain of just zero point zero one percent. Two headlines dominated the Microsoft news flow Wednesday. First, the company confirmed plans to cut thousands of jobs in an upcoming redundancy round, with reporting from finance.yahoo.com noting the scale is expected to be smaller than the prior year's cuts. Workforce reductions of this nature are typically read by the market as margin-accretive, but the optics carry their own risks around execution and morale. Second, on a more forward-looking note, Genpact announced a new AI-powered deductions recovery solution built on Microsoft Azure, underscoring the continued commercial momentum of the Azure platform as an enterprise AI backbone. At eight point four two percent of this portfolio, M-S-F-T's near-flat close is not a concern for one session — but the combination of macro rate uncertainty and workforce restructuring headlines bears watching.

NVIDIA, N-V-D-A, closed at two hundred dollars and nine cents, up seven dollars and fifty-six cents, a gain of three point nine three percent. There was no company-specific catalyst driving the move — N-V-D-A rode the session's broad AI infrastructure enthusiasm. The macro risk, however, was front and center Wednesday. Reporting from finance.yahoo.com highlighted that Federal Reserve officials now believe higher interest rates may be necessary to curb inflation, a historically negative signal for high-multiple growth stocks. Additionally, the removal of the Fed's forward guidance framework by Chair Warsh — described by finance.yahoo.com as taking away Wall Street's radar — adds duration risk to positions like N-V-D-A that carry elevated valuations. At eight point one five percent of the portfolio, Wednesday was a strong session, but the rate backdrop warrants close attention.

Amazon, A-M-Z-N, closed at two hundred thirty-eight dollars and thirty-four cents, up five dollars and sixty-five cents, a gain of two point four three percent. No company-specific news drove Wednesday's move. The advance tracked broad consumer discretionary and cloud strength. At five point five two percent portfolio weight, A-M-Z-N remains a steady contributor.

Broadcom, A-V-G-O, closed at three hundred seventy-seven dollars and seventy-five cents, up twelve dollars and seventy-three cents, a gain of three point four nine percent. No standalone news. A-V-G-O continues to benefit from the same AI semiconductor infrastructure tailwind that powered N-V-D-A on Wednesday. At four point six one percent of the portfolio, it was a clean, quiet outperformer.

Meanwhile, Meta Platforms, M-E-T-A, closed at five hundred sixty-three dollars and twenty-nine cents, up thirteen dollars and four cents, a gain of two point three seven percent. No specific news drove the session. M-E-T-A continues to benefit from its positioning in digital advertising and AI-driven engagement. At four point four eight percent of the portfolio, the move was consistent with the broader communication services strength that also lifted G-O-O-G-L.

Now the session's standout performer. Tesla, T-S-L-A, closed at four hundred twenty dollars and sixty cents, up forty dollars and eighty-nine cents — a gain of ten point seven seven percent on Wednesday's session. That is a significant single-day move for any megacap name. No specific news item in Wednesday's feed explained the catalyst directly, which suggests either a short squeeze dynamic, a broader momentum rotation into high-beta names, or market-moving news that broke after the primary data cut. At three point five four percent of the portfolio, T-S-L-A's move was the single largest dollar contributor to Wednesday's gains. Watch for follow-through or reversal signals in Thursday's pre-market.

Finally, Costco, C-O-S-T, was the lone decliner Wednesday. The stock closed at nine hundred thirty-five dollars and forty-seven cents, down seventeen dollars and seven cents, a loss of one point seven nine percent. No company-specific news was cited. C-O-S-T often trades as a defensive anchor in this portfolio, and Wednesday's rotation into high-growth, high-beta technology names likely drew capital away from the consumer staples space. At two point eight one percent weight, the pullback is modest in portfolio impact terms.

Three data points to watch in the next forty-eight hours. First, any follow-up statement from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh regarding the removal of forward guidance — the bond market is repricing in real time, and further commentary could move technology valuations sharply. Second, watch for a formal ruling from South Korea's Korea Fair Trade Commission on the Google, G-O-O-G-L, Android app store case — the financial penalty figure could move when the final order is issued. Third, monitor any EU Commission response to the Apple, A-A-P-L, Cook-Virkkunen call — a constructive signal there could unlock Siri AI deployment in a major market.

That is your Wednesday recap for July 1 2026. I am Josh Weinberg. The next episode of The Portfolio Podcast drops at 5 PM ET on the next trading day. The rate outlook is the story to watch — how the market prices the end of Fed forward guidance will set the tone for everything in this portfolio heading deeper into the second half of twenty twenty-six.
